data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: Chinese stocks, HPQ, SOUN, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher on Friday after the January personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index, the Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation gauge, came in line with expectations.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • SoundHound (NASDAQ:SOUN) +16% – Shares surged after Q4 results beat estimates, with revenue rising 101% Y/Y. Strong year-end performance propelled the company to the top end of its revenue guidance range, driven by momentum in voice-enabled Agentic AI. SoundHound ended the year with nearly $200 million in cash and no debt. The company raised its full-year 2025 revenue outlook to a range of $157M-$177M (midpoint: $167M), up from the prior $155M-$175M range and above the $165.27M consensus.
  • Perrigo (PRGO) +18% – Shares soared after the company unveiled its strategic plan at its Virtual Investor Day, despite reporting mixed Q4 results. The plan emphasizes consumerization, simplification, and scaling operations to drive long-term growth. For FY 2025, Perrigo targets all-in net sales growth of 1% to 3%, organic net sales growth of 2.5% to 4.5%, and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.90 to $3.10. The company reaffirmed its commitment to shareholder returns, maintaining 23 consecutive years of dividend payments with a 4.81% yield. Looking ahead, Perrigo projects 100% operating cash flow conversion to adjusted net income, free cash flow at ~6% of net sales, and an organic net sales CAGR of 2.5% to 4.5% from 2025 to 2027, alongside margin improvements.

Biggest stock losers

  • HP (NYSE:HPQ) -4% – Shares dipped after the company reported FQ1 results, showing a 2% Y/Y decline in its printing segment. For FY2025, HP expects earnings per share between $3.45 and $3.75, with a midpoint of $3.60, above the $3.57 consensus. However, its FQ2 EPS forecast of $0.75 to $0.85, with a midpoint of $0.80, fell short of the $0.86 consensus. The company also stated that by the end of FY2025, over 90% of its products sold in North America will be manufactured outside of China.
  • Opendoor Technologies (NASDAQ:OPEN) -6% – Shares dropped after Q4 results showed a sequential topline decline and a wider loss, along with a weak outlook. The online real estate platform expects Q1 2025 revenue between $1.0B and $1.075B, falling short of the $1.33B consensus. It also forecasts a contribution profit of $40M to $50M, below the $64.4M Visible Alpha estimate, and adjusted EBITDA ranging from -$50M to -$40M, vs. the -$29.3M Visible Alpha estimate.
  • Rocket Lab USA (NASDAQ:RKLB) -13% – Shares plunged on a soft outlook despite a record-setting year, with annual revenue reaching $436.2M, up 78% Y/Y, and a record Q4 revenue of $132.4M, up 121% Y/Y. The company expects Q1 2025 revenue between $117M and $123M, below the $135.67M consensus, and an adjusted EBITDA loss between $33M and $35M.
  • Duolingo (NASDAQ:DUOL) -9% – Shares slipped despite strong results and an upbeat outlook. The language learning platform reported robust user growth, with daily active users surging 51% Y/Y to 40.5M, monthly active users rising 32% to 116.7M, and paid subscribers growing 43% to 9.5M. CEO Luis von Ahn highlighted the success of Duolingo Max, the highest subscription tier, in driving growth. For Q1 2025, the company expects revenue between $220.5M and $223.5M, with a midpoint of $222M, above the $220.76M consensus, while full-year revenue is projected at $962.5M to $978.5M, with a midpoint of $970.5M, exceeding the $964.22M consensus.
  • NetApp (NASDAQ:NTAP) -13% – Shares plunged after the tech firm reported lower-than-expected FQ3 revenue and issued a downbeat outlook. The data management and storage solutions company expects FQ4 adjusted EPS between $1.84 and $1.94, with a midpoint of $1.89, missing the $2.00 estimate. For FY2026, NetApp forecasts adjusted EPS of $7.17 to $7.27, with a midpoint of $7.22, well below the $7.90 consensus.
  • U.S.-listed Chinese stocks tumbled after President Donald Trump announced he would impose an additional 10% tariff on Chinese goods starting March 4. In response, China’s Ministry of Commerce stated it “firmly opposes” the decision and will take necessary countermeasures if implemented. The renewed trade tensions weighed on major Chinese companies, with Alibaba (BABA) down 4%, PDD Holdings (PDD) and Nio (NIO) down 4% each, Li Auto (LI) down 5%, XPeng (XPEV) down 6%, Weibo (WB) down 3.6%, and Baidu (BIDU) down 2%. Investors remain cautious as they assess the potential economic impact of rising U.S.-China trade friction..

data.title: Citi accidentally credits account with $81T in 'near miss' - report
data.description:
Citigroup (NYSE:C) erroneously credited a client's account with $81T in what should have been a $280 transaction, a mistake that could extend regulators' scrutiny as the bank attempts to convince them that it has fixed operational issues, according to a media report.

The internal transfer error occurred in April 2024 and was missed by a payments employee and a second Citi (NYSE:C) official tasked with double-checking the transaction before it was approved and processed at the beginning of the next business day, the Financial Times reported on Friday, citing a Citi internal account of the episode.

Another employee flagged a discrepancy in the bank's account balances about 90 minutes after the payment was posted, the report said. It was reversed several hours later.

The error was disclosed to the Federal Reserve as a "near miss," as no funds actually left Citi (C) and didn't impact the bank or its client, the FT said.

An internal report chronicled 10 near misses of $1B or more in the last year, which was down from 13 in the previous year, the article said. A near miss is when a bank processes the wrong amount, but is able to recover the funds.

Citi (C) was already a focus of regulator scrutiny after it erroneously sent $900M to creditors in July 2020, paying off the entire amount owed to Revlon creditors instead of the interest payment that was intended. Citi went to court to try to claw back the amounts from the creditors, as several refused to voluntarily hand the payments back. The case was dismissed in late 2022 after the creditors eventually agreed to return the money.

In July 2024, the company was fined $135.6M by two U.S. regulators for failing to make enough progress in fixing its data quality management problems. At the time, CEO Jane Fraser said, "As we’ve said from the beginning of this multi-year effort, we’re committed to spending what is necessary to address our consent orders, as our agreement with the OCC demonstrates."

data.title: U.S. stocks seesaw post open, tick slightly higher after in-line inflation data
data.description:
U.S. stocks looked for direction on Friday, with market participants receiving a key inflation reading that came in-line with expectations.

After opening mixed, the major averages have seesawed. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.3% in morning trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was flat. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.6%.

U.S. Treasury yields were lower. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 3 basis points to 4.24%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 3 basis points to 4.04%.

Before the open, the January Core PCE Price Index came in at +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% expected and +0.2% prior, and +2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.9% (revised from +2.8%).

PCE Price Index, which includes food and energy prices, was +0.3% M/M vs. +0.4% consensus and +0.3% prior, and +2.5% Y/Y vs. +2.5% consensus and +2.6% prior.

Wall Street caught a bout of heavy selling in the final hour of trading on Thursday, as sentiment took a hit from a post-earnings fall in semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) and more tariff-related moves by U.S. President Donald Trump.

"Markets were in a stressed mood of their own yesterday, with renewed tariff threats from Trump in addition to a sharp tech sell-off that saw the Magnificent 7 post its worst day of 2025," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

"Equity markets reacted to social media posts from US President Trump. Trump is often more coherent on social media than in off-the-cuff comments, and these remarks made clear an intention to hike taxes—US buyers will need 25% more cash to buy Canada or Mexico, and another 10% cash to buy from China," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: BridgeBio spinout to go public in SPAC deal with Helix Acquisition Corp. II
data.description:
BridgeBio Oncology Therapeutics, which was spun out of BridgeBio Pharma (NASDAQ:BBIO) last year, has agreed to merge with Helix Acquisition Corp. II (NASDAQ:HLXB), a Boston-headquartered SPAC, to become a publicly traded biotech, the companies announced Friday.

The combined company, named BridgeBio Oncology, is expected to trade on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol BBOT.

The transaction is expected to generate $550M in gross proceeds, including about $196M held in Helix Acquisition (NASDAQ:HLXB) and BridgeBio’s $100M cash at closing. Meanwhile, roughly $260M will be raised from a concurrent PIPE financing.

BridgeBio (BBOT) intends to use net proceeds to advance its three lead candidates, BBO-8520, BBO-10203, and BBO-11818, which are still in the early stages of development against multiple cancer types.

“This financing and transaction mark a significant milestone for our company. We are profoundly grateful to the patients who participate in our trials, our dedicated BBOT team members, and our investors,” CEO Eli Wallace remarked.

BridgeBio Oncology Therapeutics was launched in May 2024 following a $200M private financing with external investors.

data.title: Intel delays finish of Ohio chip plant until at least 2030: report
data.description:
• Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) has delayed the completion date of its $20B semiconductor manufacturing plant in Ohio until at least 2030, The Columbus Dispatch reported.
  • The Santa Clara, Calif.-based chip giant told the news outlet that the end date that the first factory in New Albany, Ohio would not be completed until 2030. Initial operations would then start either later that year or in the early part of 2031, the news outlet added.
  • A second factory in the Ohio city is not expected to be completed until the early 2030s.
  • Seeking Alpha has reached out to Intel to understand more about the delay.
  • The new delay is the second time that the struggling chip has pushed back the timeline on its Ohio plant. It was reported last February that Intel had expected the manufacturing plants to be completed by late 2026. At the time, Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon said the delay was more about not needing the capacity anytime soon and less about technological issues.
  • Intel announced its plans for the manufacturing facility near Columbus, Ohio in January 2022. The land for the facility was acquired in July 2022.

data.title: Microsoft to shut down Skype, seeks to move users to Teams
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) said on Friday it is pulling the plug on its two-decade-old calling and messaging service Skype, marking the end of an era.

The tech giant, which acquired Skype 14 years ago for $8.5 billion, said it will be retiring Skype in May 2025 and is encouraging users to migrate to its free Teams app.

Founded in 2003, Skype came into attention long before Zoom and WhatsApp, as it helped people to connect globally at a time when international phone calls were expensive. Moving forward, it was believed that Skype would again dominate the video-calling space during the pandemic as people started working from home.

However, intense competition from rivals including Zoom and Slack in recent years led Skype to lose steam, as it failed to provide a compelling mobile experience.

“Skype has been an integral part of shaping modern communications and supporting countless meaningful moments, and we are honored to have been part of the journey,” said Jeff Teper, President of Microsoft’s Collaborative Apps and Platforms.

The company said over the coming days, it will roll out the ability for Skype users to sign in to Teams on any supported device using their Skype credentials, while their chats and contacts will automatically appear in the app, so users can quickly pick up where they left off.

During this transition period, Teams users can call and chat with Skype users and vice versa, said Microsoft.

Microsoft said it will no longer offer paid Skype features to new customers, including Skype Credit and subscriptions that allow people to make and receive international and domestic calls.

The company said the move will help it to streamline its free consumer communications offerings.

“We’re excited about the new opportunities that Teams brings and are committed to helping you stay connected in new and meaningful ways,” added Teper.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson’s DARZALEX SC gets positive CHMP opinion
data.description:
Janssen-Cilag International NV, a Johnson & Johnson company (NYSE:JNJ) announced Friday that an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA), recommended DARZALEX for multiple myeloma treatment.

Issuing a so-called positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended the marketing authorization for daratumumab SC in combination with bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone for the treatment of newly diagnosed multiple myeloma, which is a cancer of the bone marrow.

data.title: Could Sycamore acquire Walgreens below market value? - Deutsche Bank
data.description:
Sycamore Partners’ potential bid to take Walgreens Boots Alliance (NASDAQ:WBA) private and split into three parts seems “highly difficult” which leads Deutsche Bank’s George Hill to see a more likely outcome of a “take-under” or for Sycamore to abandon the deal altogether. Accordingly, Hill views the deal with an “unusually high degree of uncertainty that makes it much less straightforward than a regular leveraged buyout.”

For this reason, Hill downgrades Walgreens Boots Alliance (NASDAQ:WBA) to Sell from Hold and cuts his target price by 18% to $9.

When Deutsche Bank runs its LBO model on a WBA deal, the model generates a price that is less than the current share price.

“From a post-deal break-up perspective, we question how one apportions liability between the three companies, and we suspect debt investors would want to know that before investing,” Hill says, noting the opioid and tax liabilities would reside with the U.S. retail business.

The challenges lie not only with a Sycamore transaction or pending litigations, but within the company itself as Walgreens (NASDAQ:WBA) faces increased competition, labor shortages and no viable plan to address these issues.

“Walgreens Boots Alliance's latest earnings report showed that the company's new CEO, Tim Wentworth, is still facing significant challenges in dealing with the two biggest issues for the retailer -- continued margin compression and falling earnings,” Seeking Alpha investor group Skeptical12 says.

Skeptical12 also recommends investors sell Walgreens (WBA) stock, citing the stock’s overvaluation, structural challenges in the retail pharmacy industry, and pending opioid litigation that will adversely affect Walgreens (WBA) Pharmacy Benefit Manager business model.

“This stock still looks overvalued with significant downside risk,” the investment group adds. It should be noted Skeptical12’s report was written prior to a 26% appreciation in the stock price since February 11.

Seeking Alpha authors and Wall Street analysts all have a consensus rating of Hold on Walgreens (WBA), with Seeking Alpha’s Quant score of 3.36 out of 5 also putting the stock as a Hold.

After climbing as much as 7% on the Sycamore news Thursday, Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA) shares have retreated by 5% off yesterday’s close.

data.title: AMD unveils next-gen graphics cards for gamers challenging Nvidia dominance
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) unveiled its AMD RDNA 4 graphics architecture with the launch of the AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT and RX 9070 graphics cards.

The products, which are part of the Radeon RX 9000 Series, feature 16GB of memory and improvements for high-quality gaming graphics, including re-vamped raytracing accelerators and AI accelerators for high performance, and breakthrough gaming experiences, according to AMD.

Last month, market leader Nvidia (NVDA) unveiled its most advanced consumer graphics processing units, or GPUs, for gamers, creators and developers — the GeForce RTX 50 Series Desktop and Laptop GPUs powered by Nvidia Blackwell.

"Through the power of advanced AI and Raytracing accelerators, we're not just improving frame rates – we're fundamentally enhancing the gaming experience. Offering incredible performance, AI-powered features, and next-gen display support at competitive price points, the Radeon RX 9000 Series delivers exceptional value for gamers looking to upgrade their systems," said David McAfee, Corporate VP and General Manager, Ryzen CPU and Radeon Graphics AMD.

The company noted that the new graphics cards are expected to be available from March 6. The Radeon RX 9070 XT has a price of $599, while the Radeon RX 9070 carries a price tag of $549.
